title: Dunkirk Evacuation (Operation Dynamo)
summary: Operation Dynamo was the Allied evacuation from Dunkirk following the German breakthrough at Sedan. Over nine days, 338,226 Allied soldiers were lifted from the beaches and harbour by a fleet of warships and civilian vessels — though all heavy equipment was abandoned.

axis.strength: Army Group A Panzers encircling the perimeter

key_event: The controversial 'Halt Order' of 23–26 May, attributed to Hitler and supported by Rundstedt, paused the Panzer advance short of Dunkirk — a decision that allowed the evacuation to proceed. Whether deliberate or operational, it remains one of the war's great mysteries.
outcome: Allied strategic success despite tactical defeat. 338,226 troops evacuated; 35,000–40,000 French troops captured. All Allied heavy equipment abandoned. Churchill famously cautioned that 'wars are not won by evacuations.'
